obsidian_markdown:
# A Systematic Literature Review on Machine and Deep Learning Approaches for Detecting Attacks in RPL-Based 6LoWPAN of Internet of Things

## Summary
This paper conducts a systematic literature review on machine and deep learning approaches for detecting attacks in RPL-based 6LoWPAN within the Internet of Things (IoT). It highlights the vulnerabilities of 6LoWPAN nodes due to their limited resources, making them susceptible to various threats. The review categorizes and analyzes existing machine and deep learning techniques applied to enhance the security of RPL-based networks, identifying gaps and suggesting future research directions.

## Contributions
- A comprehensive review of machine and deep learning approaches for attack detection in RPL-based 6LoWPAN networks.
- Identification of the main types of attacks targeting these networks and the corresponding detection methods.
- Highlighting the limitations of current approaches and suggesting directions for future research.

## Method
The paper employs a systematic literature review methodology, searching and analyzing existing academic publications on machine and deep learning techniques for attack detection in RPL-based 6LoWPAN networks. The approach involves categorizing the findings based on the types of attacks and detection methods used.

## Results
The review identifies several machine and deep learning methods applied to detect different types of attacks in RPL-based 6LoWPAN networks. It outlines the strengths and weaknesses of each method, providing a detailed comparison of their effectiveness and applicability.

## Limitations
The review notes limitations such as the limited number of studies specifically addressing RPL-based 6LoWPAN networks and the need for more empirical validation of the proposed methods. Future work should focus on developing more robust models and conducting extensive real-world testing.

## Keywords
Internet of Things, RPL, 6LoWPAN, Machine Learning, Deep Learning, Attack Detection, Security, Systematic Review
